content.problemStatement:
There are NNN cities connected by roads such that they form a tree-like structure.

Your task is to build exactly AiA_iAi‚Äã towers in ithi^{th}ith city. One or multiple towers can be built in a city only if there is a brick factory present in that city.

Currently, only city 111 has a brick factory. To complete the project, you can do the following two types of operations:


Type 111: Construct a tower in a city that already has a brick factory.
Type 222: Construct a brick factory in a city such that any of its neighbouring cities already have a brick factory.

Note that two cities are considered as neighbouring cities if there is a road connecting them.
Find the number of **different ways** to complete the project such that the total number of operations performed is **minimum**. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note: Two ways are considered different if the order and type of operations is not exactly the same for both ways.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of cities.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the number of towers to be built in ithi^{th}ith city.
The next N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 lines describe the roads. The ithi^{th}ith of these N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 lines contain two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã, denoting a road between city uiu_iui‚Äã and city viv_ivi‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of **different ways** to complete the project in **minimum cost** modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Two ways are considered different if the order and type of operations is not exactly the same for both ways.
constraints: The sum of AiA_iAi‚Äã over any test case is at least 111 and won't exceed 10610^6106.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

BiB_iBi‚Äã represents construction of a brick factory in the city iii.
TiT_iTi‚Äã represents construction of a tower in the city iii.

Test case 2: There are 333 possible ways to complete this project in minimum number of operations: B2T2T1B_2 T_2 T_1B2‚ÄãT2‚ÄãT1‚Äã, B2T1T2B_2 T_1 T_2B2‚ÄãT1‚ÄãT2‚Äã and T1B2T2 T_1 B_2 T_2T1‚ÄãB2‚ÄãT2‚Äã.
Test case 3: There are 333 possible ways to complete this project in minimum number of operations: B3T3T1B_3 T_3 T_1B3‚ÄãT3‚ÄãT1‚Äã, B3T1T3B_3 T_1 T_3B3‚ÄãT1‚ÄãT3‚Äã and T1B3T3 T_1 B_3 T_3T1‚ÄãB3‚ÄãT3‚Äã.
